body.title: Mass-flip pending igor-worker tickets to claude during cert (belt-and-suspenders)
body.result: cert_worker_freeze.py — --freeze/--unfreeze/--status modes; metadata.original_worker preserves restoration; idempotent. Live freeze ran on 2 igor-worker tickets (T-wire-graph-preparse-pipeline, T-wg-cooccur-tree-restructure). 10 tests, 4213 regression pass.
body.description:
Belt-and-suspenders for the cert phase: even with IGOR_SINGLE_TICKET in place, a forgotten env var or a fresh shell would let Igor's pe_chain start picking tickets again. Pre-emptively flip every pending worker=igor ticket to worker=claude (preserving the original worker in metadata.original_worker for restoration after cert).

When cert completes: a one-line script flips them all back. Until then, every pending igor-side ticket sits as worker=claude — Igor's pe_chain filter at pe_chain.py:2704 already skips non-igor tickets.

**Affected files:** lab/claudecode/cert_worker_freeze.py (small script with --freeze and --unfreeze modes) + queue.json mutations (data-only, no schema change).
**Design rules:** none apply (queue.json mutation, scripted).
**Scope boundary:** in scope: freeze/unfreeze script + initial run. Out of scope: integration with /sprint or other workflows.
**Test plan:** unit test: freeze sets worker=claude + records original_worker in metadata; unfreeze restores worker from metadata; idempotent (re-running freeze on already-frozen is a no-op).
